rules.singleActiveFeature: Only one feature can be 'in_progress' at a time
rules.evidenceRequired: Must have verification evidence before marking 'pass'
rules.verificationMandatory: All checklist items must run and pass before marking 'pass'

statusDefinitions.in_progress: Currently being worked on (only one allowed)
statusDefinitions.blocked: Cannot proceed â€” reason documented in notes

features.title: Project Scaffold + Collections
features.description: Next.js 15 + Payload CMS 3 + SQLite setup. Collections: users, customers, products, categories, media, orders. Seed script.
verification: npm run dev starts without errors
Payload admin panel accessible at /admin
npx tsx src/seed.ts creates products and categories
evidence: Dev server starts on port 3000
Admin panel accessible, collections visible
Seed creates 4 bike products with categories

features.title: Access Control + Hooks
features.description: Reusable access functions in src/access/index.ts. Product hooks: beforeChange forces draft status, afterChange logs, beforeDelete guards.
verification: Creating product via admin sets status=draft automatically
npm run lint exits 0
evidence: beforeChange hook confirmed forcing draft on create
isSeeding context flag bypasses the hook correctly

features.title: REST & Local API

features.title: Cart Context + CartReducer

features.title: Checkout + Orders

features.title: Product Search via @payloadcms/plugin-search
features.description: Полнотекстовый поиск по продуктам. Индексирует name + description (plain text из Lexical) + category.name. UI: нативная HTML-форма на /products, submit → ?q=. Поле задизейблено для неавторизованных.

search-коллекция видима в /admin

features.notes: Три бага исправлены в ходе верификации: (1) @payloadcms/plugin-search версия 3.84.1 → 3.78.0 (версионный mismatch); (2) products/page.tsx where-запрос расширен до OR [title, meta.description, meta.categoryName]; (3) beforeSync стал async + использует payload.findByID когда category — ID (depth:0 при reindex).

features.title: Order Intake module — server-side price verification
features.description: Extract order creation logic into src/lib/orders.ts. createOrder(items, customerId) fetches product prices from DB (ignores client-provided price), validates items array, calculates total server-side, creates Order via Payload Local API. checkout/route.ts becomes a thin HTTP adapter.
verification: npm run lint exits 0
npm run test:int exits 0
POST /api/checkout with tampered price → order.total uses DB price, not client price
POST /api/checkout with non-existent productId → 400 error
POST /api/checkout with items=null or items not array → 400 error
POST /api/checkout with valid items → order created, total matches DB prices × quantities
src/lib/orders.ts exports createOrder() directly callable via Payload Local API
evidence: npm run lint: PASS (1 pre-existing warning: unused req in afterChange.ts)
npm run test:int: PASS (1 test, 3s)
POST /api/checkout with items=null → 400 {error: 'items must be a non-empty array'}
POST /api/checkout with items=[] → 400 {error: 'items must be a non-empty array'}
POST /api/checkout with productId=99999 → 400 {error: 'Product not found: 99999'}
POST /api/checkout with tampered price=1, productId=11 (dbPrice=249900) → 201, order.total=249900 (DB price used, client price ignored)
POST /api/checkout without auth → 401
src/lib/orders.ts exports createOrder() and ValidationError
features.notes: Deepening opportunity #1 from architecture review. Security fix: client-provided prices replaced with DB prices. Seam: createOrder() is the new interface for order creation business logic. Three-step fix: (1) extract createOrder() to src/lib/orders.ts; (2) add ValidationError class for 400 vs 500 distinction; (3) wrap payload.findByID in try/catch because Payload throws (not returns null) on NotFound.

features.title: Production Deployment — Vercel + Neon + Vercel Blob + Resend
features.description: Switch from SQLite to Neon Postgres (db-postgres adapter). Add Vercel Blob for media storage (storage-vercel-blob). Replace Ethereal with Resend email adapter. Deploy to Vercel free tier. Stripe test keys remain.
verification: npm run lint exits 0
npm run build exits 0 (Next.js production build)
Vercel deployment succeeds (no build errors)
Payload admin panel accessible at prod URL
npx tsx src/seed.ts runs successfully against Neon DB
Media upload in admin saves to Vercel Blob (not local disk)
Stripe checkout completes and order flips to paid status
evidence: npm run lint: PASS (pre-existing warnings only)
Vercel build: PASS — https://payload-start-seven.vercel.app
Payload admin accessible at /admin
seed.ts: 6 products created in Neon DB
Images served from Vercel Blob (not local disk)
Stripe payment completed → order status = Оплачен, cart cleared
VERCEL_URL fallback added to checkout route (no manual NEXT_PUBLIC_SERVER_URL needed)
Production Stripe webhook registered for checkout.session.completed
features.notes: Decisions: Vercel Blob (media), Neon direct, @payloadcms/db-postgres adapter, Resend email, Stripe test keys, vercel.app domain. Fixes applied: initial migration via npm run ci, importMap regenerated, seed _status:published explicit, baseUrl VERCEL_URL fallback, production webhook whsec registered.

features.title: Stripe Checkout integration (hosted)
features.description: Replace toast-based checkout with real Stripe Hosted Checkout. Order created BEFORE payment with status=pending, Stripe Session linked via stripeSessionId field, webhook flips status→paid on checkout.session.completed. Frontend cart redirects to session.url; /orders/[id] shows order state.
verification: npm run lint exits 0
npm run test:int exits 0 (incl. stripe-checkout.int.spec.ts from Phase 6)
stripeSessionId field visible on Order docs in /admin and in payload-types.ts
POST /api/checkout returns { checkoutUrl, orderId } and persists session.id on the order
POST /api/webhooks/stripe with valid signature flips order pending → paid
POST /api/webhooks/stripe with invalid signature returns 400
Webhook is idempotent (replay does not double-process)
Cart 'Оформить заказ' redirects to Stripe-hosted page; cancel returns to /cart
/orders/[id] renders items/total/status (pending/paid) and gates unauthenticated access
evidence: Phase 1: stripeSessionId text+indexed+readOnly added to Orders collection; payload-types.ts regenerated with stripeSessionId?: string | null on Order interface

Phase 2: stripe@22.1.1 installed; src/lib/stripe.ts (singleton with STRIPE_SECRET_KEY guard); checkout/route.ts creates Order(pending) → Stripe Session → writes stripeSessionId → returns {checkoutUrl, orderId}; metadata.orderId attached as webhook fallback

Phase 3: src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts with runtime=nodejs, raw body via req.text(), constructEvent signature verify, primary lookup by stripeSessionId + fallback by metadata.orderId, idempotent (paid→200 short-circuit), 200 OK on unknown event types
Phase 4: cart/page.tsx redirects via window.location.href = checkoutUrl, disables both buttons during in-flight, does NOT clearCart on session create (cancel-to-/cart preserves items)

Phase 5: /orders/[id]/page.tsx — async RSC, params/searchParams as Promise (Next 15), auth-gated via getAuthenticatedUser, findByID with overrideAccess:false (foreign order → 404), green 'Оплата прошла успешно' banner on session_id + status=paid, neutral 'Платёж обрабатывается' banner on session_id + status=pending; ClearCartOnPaid client component mounted inside the paid banner clears cart exactly once
Phase 5 bonus: /orders list statusConfig synced with current schema (pending/paid/shipped/cancelled — removed stale confirmed/delivered)

Phase 6: tests/int/stripe-checkout.int.spec.ts — 15 tests across 3 describe blocks (createOrder 8, webhook 6, checkout auth-gate 1). Real Payload + DB, signed webhook requests via stripe.webhooks.generateTestHeaderString, Stripe SDK partially mocked (real webhooks + stubbed sessions.create). All 16 tests in suite pass (15 new + 1 baseline) in ~10s, bash init.sh PASS
features.notes: Branch: feat/stripe-checkout (off main). Env vars in .env: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOKS_SIGNING_SECRET. Local e2e: `stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks/stripe`, test card 4242 4242 4242 4242. Bug fixed mid-session: ClearCartOnPaid had [clearCart] in deps → infinite loop (clearCart recreated each CartProvider render); fixed to [] + eslint-disable. Open question: webhook handler swallows DB errors → 200 to avoid Stripe retries — acceptable for portfolio, may want to return 500 in prod for transient DB failures.

features.title: Internationalization — next-intl + Payload localization (RU/EN)
features.description: Multilingual storefront across UI chrome (next-intl message catalogs) and content (Payload localization on Products.name/description, Categories.name, SiteSettings). Locale-prefixed routing /ru /en — app/(frontend) moves under app/[locale]/, middleware added, products/@sidebar parallel route carried along. RU is the default locale; EN is a translation; missing content falls back to RU (fallback: true). URL locale is the single source of truth and drives the locale param passed to Payload Local API queries. See docs/adr/0002.
verification: npm run lint exits 0
npm run build exits 0
Locale switcher toggles /ru <-> /en, preserving the current path
UI strings change language via next-intl catalogs
Product/category/banner content shows EN when present, falls back to RU when EN missing
Payload admin shows per-locale field tabs for localized fields
products/@sidebar still renders under the [locale] segment
evidence: npm run build: PASS (exit 0, 13/13 static pages; only pre-existing no-unused-vars warnings in generated migration files)
npx tsc --noEmit: clean
node src/scripts/check-i18n-keys.mjs: ru/en key sets identical (no drift)
Locale switcher (src/components/LocaleSwitcher.tsx) toggles /ru<->/en via router.replace(pathname,{locale}), preserving path+query; locale read from URL segment
UI strings localized via next-intl across header, nav, search, cart, auth, orders, contacts, home, PDP, catalog, sidebar, gallery, add-to-cart (messages/ru.json + en.json)
Localized Payload fields: Products.description, Categories.name (localized:true); locale passed into payload.find/findByID on PDP, catalog, categories, categories/[slug], @sidebar
Content verified per-locale on dev + prod: products_locales ru=6/en=6, categories_locales ru=3/en=3; category names Шоссейные/Гравел/Туринг <-> Road/Gravel/Touring
Payload admin shows per-locale tabs for localized fields (confirmed visually)
products/@sidebar renders under [locale] segment with localized category names
features.notes: First feature in the grilled batch; foundation for #19 and #20. Default locale RU, EN translation, fallback:true. Prod deploy gotcha: localized-field schema was added via dev push, so prod (migrate-based) lacked *_locales tables; Vercel `payload migrate` silently skipped because a batch:-1 dev-push marker triggered an interactive data-loss prompt that CI can't answer. Fixed with `payload migrate:create localization` (committed migration) + one-time `payload migrate:fresh` on prod + reseed. Hardened seed.ts to force PAYLOAD_MIGRATING=true (seeds never push schema). Lesson: don't mix dev push with prod migrations.

features.title: Customer social + magic-link auth (Payload-native)
features.description: Add sign-in methods for Customer: magic link (email), Google OAuth, Yandex ID OAuth. Payload-native: customers collection stays the single source of truth; OAuth/magic-link flows find-or-create a customer by verified email and issue the existing customer-token JWT. Email/password stays enabled (additive). getAuthenticatedUser and order access scoping unchanged. See docs/adr/0001.
verification: npm run lint exits 0
npm run test:int exits 0
Magic-link email sends; clicking link signs in and sets customer-token
Google sign-in find-or-creates a customer by verified email and sets customer-token
Yandex sign-in find-or-creates a customer by verified email (login:email scope) and sets customer-token
Same verified email via different methods resolves to the SAME customer (no duplicates)
Linking only occurs when provider asserts email_verified
Existing email/password login still works
evidence: npm run lint: exit 0 (only pre-existing req/payload warnings in migration files)
npm run test:int: 5 files, 38 tests pass — incl. tests/int/magic-link.int.spec.ts (10) + tests/int/oauth.int.spec.ts (5)

Magic-link e2e: real Resend email received at 3334976@gmail.com, link signed in, customer-token set, email shown in header. Tested: hashed ~15min token, one-time-use, expiry, missing/unknown token, no account enumeration (always 200)
Google sign-in e2e: live consent -> callback find-or-creates customer by email_verified email -> customer-token set (verified locally)
Yandex sign-in e2e: live consent (login:email+login:info) -> callback uses default_email -> customer-token set (verified locally)
Same email -> same customer: unit-tested in oauth.int.spec.ts (linkVerifiedCustomer resolves existing customer, no duplicate) + magic-link.int.spec.ts
email_verified gate: linkVerifiedCustomer throws OAuthEmailNotVerifiedError when !emailVerified || !email; Google requires email_verified===true, Yandex requires default_email presence
Email/password login unchanged (additive); getAuthenticatedUser + orders scoping untouched
Independent security-auditor verdict: PASS (no Critical/High). Commits: a9bc361 (migration+dev-fix), a5d2695 (magic tests), bd5c479 (OAuth)
features.notes: Done. arctic v3 for OAuth handshake. Stateless customer JWT (useSessions:false) -> dropped customers_sessions, added magic_tokens (migration 20260629_083137). Security follow-ups (non-blocking, from audit): (1) Medium - add rate-limit to POST /api/auth/magic/request (email-bombing); (2) Low - atomic magic-token consumption (TOCTOU); (3) Low - clear oauth cookies on callback fail path; (4) Low - getBaseUrl trusts Host header for magic-link URL, prefer NEXT_PUBLIC_SERVER_URL in prod. Prod: register https redirect URIs in Google/Yandex consoles + set 4 env vars in Vercel + redeploy; mind Google consent Testing mode + Vercel Deployment Protection.

features.title: Homepage — Payload Blocks + animated hero (motion)
features.description: Expand homepage. Hero stays a bespoke client-component island with interactive animation via `motion` (formerly framer-motion); page.tsx remains a server component (RSC fetches blocks/settings). New sections below become a small Payload Blocks builder (2-3 block types, e.g. feature-grid / CTA / featured-products) on a Homepage global, localized via #18. CONTEXT.md: Hero is NOT a Content block.
verification: npm run lint exits 0
npm run build exits 0
Hero animation runs client-side; page.tsx stays an RSC (no 'use client' at page level)
Admin can add/reorder Content blocks on the Homepage global
Block content renders localized per current locale with RU fallback
Hero respects prefers-reduced-motion
evidence: npm run build: PASS (exit 0, static+dynamic routes, only pre-existing migration-file warnings); npm run check:i18n: ru/en parity (no key drift)

Phase 1 (00ca7bd): new global `homepage` (src/globals/Homepage.ts) with 3 block types feature-grid/cta/contacts, field-level localized:true on text fields (#18 pattern); registered in payload.config.ts; motion@12.42 installed; payload-types regenerated with Homepage type

Phase 2 (bd71a39): committed migration 20260630_061057_homepage_blocks (creates homepage + homepage_locales); dev migration history reconciled (batch:-1 marker removed, 3 prior migrations marked applied) per memory — NO migrate:fresh, NO dev-push to prod; `npx payload migrate` applied only the new migration

Phase 3 (417ced1): RSC renderers src/components/blocks/{FeatureGridBlock,CtaBlock,ContactsBlock,BlocksRenderer}; page.tsx stays RSC, fetches homepage global by locale (RU fallback); ContactsBlock single-sources contact data from site-settings; removed hardcoded feature cards + home.feat* i18n keys
Phase 4 (c107d74): Hero extracted to 'use client' src/components/Hero.tsx, motion/react entrance stagger + ambient, useReducedMotion gate; page.tsx confirmed no top-level 'use client'
Phase 5 (0a15759): seed.ts populates homepage global RU+EN (read-back block-ID pattern keeps both locales' blocks in sync — 3 blocks each, IDs match across locales); verified via findGlobal ru/en
Runtime: fresh dev server (localhost:3001) serves /ru and /en → 200 (the 500 on :3000 was a stale pre-branch server)
Panel review: code-review minor, performance green, security minor, design no diff-attributable critical (only pre-existing header-nav mobile overflow)

Panel fix (be92d8b): reduced-motion now gated at CSS layer too (@media prefers-reduced-motion disables dot-pulse/fade-in; duplicate CSS reveal-up removed from motion-controlled hero elements); EN seed preserves non-localized icon; javascript:/scheme-allowlist guard on CtaBlock buttonHref + ContactsBlock social urls; site-settings fetched once (threaded page.tsx→BlocksRenderer→ContactsBlock)
features.notes: Done via /ship (6 commits on feat/homepage-blocks-animated-hero). Grilled scope: hero stays code-owned island; blocks = feature-grid + CTA + contacts (NO featured-products/categories — user rejected products-on-homepage). Lesson: Payload localized `blocks` — a bare second-locale updateGlobal creates NEW block rows and orphans the first locale; must read back block IDs after the RU write and pass them into the EN write. Prod deploy remains a manual post-merge step (CI `payload migrate` applies homepage_blocks on clean prod history; then reseed).

features.title: Homepage motion redesign — Lenis scroll + gamified hero (motion)
features.description: Substantial hero + homepage motion redesign after user rejected the prior hero as visually too weak. Direction: motion-design + gamified (Figma 2026 trends 6+7), sofi-style smooth scroll (Lenis) + jitter kinetic accents, cycling theme. Signature = SVG bike-wheel that rotates on scroll (useScroll) and doubles as spin-to-reveal. Kinetic split-text heading, odometer count-up stats, ride-meter scroll-progress bar, magnetic hover on CTAs+cards, site-wide staggered scroll reveals. Pure animation layer — NO Payload schema change. prefers-reduced-motion disables all; Lenis off on mobile/touch.
verification: npm run build exits 0
npm run check:i18n parity
page.tsx / layout / BlocksRenderer / FeatureGridBlock stay RSC (client boundary only at leaf motion components)
prefers-reduced-motion disables all motion (wheel static, text visible, stats final, no stuck opacity:0)
no React hydration mismatch on the hero wheel
evidence: npm run build: PASS (exit 0); check:i18n: ru/en parity
Phase 1 (bdfd77d): lenis SmoothScroll provider (gated prefers-reduced-motion + coarse-pointer/<1024px, RAF+destroy cleanup) + reusable <Reveal> (whileInView once, immediate on reduced-motion)

Phase 2 (677b847): Hero.tsx rewrite — scroll-driven inline-SVG ride-wheel (useScroll→useTransform rotate), masked kinetic split-text (springy accent overshoot), odometer count-up (useInView); all reduced-motion gated; page.tsx stays RSC

Phase 3 (e0c9a33): gamified — RideMeter (fixed scaleX scroll-progress), Magnetic wrapper on hero CTAs + feature cards (children pattern, cards stay RSC), spin-to-reveal on the wheel (click → static perk chip)
Phase 4 (348070a): BlocksRenderer wraps each block in <Reveal delay={idx*0.08}> for staggered scroll reveal

Panel: code-review green (3 minor), security green (lenis legit, no injection), performance minor (Magnetic useState), design SUBSTANTIAL verdict — confirmed premium redesign vs prior weak hero, reduced-motion PASS; 2 criticals raised

Panel fix (495b035): CRITICAL wheel hydration mismatch fixed (round Math.cos/sin spoke coords via toFixed(3) — verified SSR HTML emits 30.718 not 30.7179676…); RideMeter reduced-motion gate + 100vw overflow containment; Magnetic → useMotionValue+useSpring (no per-mousemove React render); removed dead sectionRef
Runtime: dev server /ru /en → 200; hydration error gone (SSR coords rounded, client uses same helper)
features.notes: Done via /ship (6 commits on feat/homepage-motion-redesign). Grilled: refs sofihealth.com + jitter.video; all 4 gamified mechanics placed in non-competing zones (spin-to-reveal consolidated into hero wheel). Follow-up (RESOLVED in PR #4, merge 3bca141): global site-header mobile overflow (923px @375, no hamburger, sub-44px targets) — fixed via responsive MobileMenu (hamburger + body-portaled off-canvas drawer). Lesson: [[ssr-svg-float-hydration]] — round SSR SVG cos/sin coords or React hydration-mismatches (runtime-only, build-green).
